LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TOUCHOFMODERN, INC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT. THE MATTER IS CALLED. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS CONTESTED LATE. THE COURT NOTES THAT OPPOSING COUNSEL IS PRESENT AND ALLOWS ARGUMENT. ARGUMENT IS HELD. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT TOUCH OF MODERN'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT IS GRANTED. (CCP 473.) THE ANSWER WILL NOT BE DEEMED FILED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR NO. 5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-29-2021 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-16-2021 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Nov-16-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Touch Of Moderns Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default, Memorandum Of Points And Authories With Exhibit A
Off Calendar May-12-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Eric Gowey, Esq. of Gowey Law, APC, 310.404.6850, for Plaintiff;
Atty. Matthew Nazareth, Esq. of Meyers Nave, 510.808.2000, for the Defendant.

DEFENDANT TOUCHOFMODERN, INC'S Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default.

The matter is called. The Court and Mr. Gowey briefly confer regarding Mr. Gowey contesting the tentative ruling late. The Court notes that opposing counsel is present and allows argument.

ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant Touch of Modern's motion to set aside entry of default is granted. (CCP 473.) The motion was filed less than three months after entry of default and our supreme court directs that "[b]ecause the law favors disposing of cases on their merits, any doubts in applying section 473 must be resolved in favor of the party seeking relief from default." (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 980.)

In response to a question by Mr. Nazareth, the Court states that the answer will not be deemed filed and notes that the court does not deem anything filed.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/RBU)
